rationale: Ground truth is a terse duplicate-closure pointing to #4808/#4809. Y explicitly reasons through and endorses that exact outcome ('closing as duplicate is the right call'), while X frames the issue as effectively resolved/confirmed-fixed on 'main' without ever naming the duplicate relationship, which is a weaker match to the actual resolution. Y is also more careful about grounding, disclosing that its checkout is the dev/vllm-align branch rather than conflating it with upstream main as X implicitly does, and it raises the legitimate open question of whether #4808 (closed, unmerged) actually landed — a nuance X glosses over by asserting the fix 'appears to have landed.' Both independently surface the same real test-coverage gap in test_kv_cache_scale_mapper.py, but Y integrates it more directly into an end-to-end answer that matches the reporter's actual situation.
